description_long: This object is a vintage television with a boxy, rectangular form and a slightly curved front screen. The casing is finished in a deep, glossy reddish-brown color with visible wear along the edges, suggesting age and frequent use. The front panel features a large, dark glass screen, two rotary dials for channel and volume control, and a speaker grille with two small push buttons beneath it. The sides and back are solid and unadorned, with the back panel showing a recessed area likely for ventilation or access to internal components. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century CRT televisions, with a sturdy, heavy build and minimalistic controls. This television would typically be found in older urban homes, garages, or as discarded electronics on city streets.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a reddish-brown glossy casing, large curved screen, and classic rotary dials on the front. The design is sturdy and shows signs of age and wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large curved screen, two rotary dials, speaker grille, and two push buttons.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the solid, slightly curved reddish-brown casing with no visible features.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a plain back panel with a recessed area, likely for ventilation or access.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with a continuous, unadorned reddish-brown surface.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, glossy reddish-brown plastic bezel with rounded edges and visible wear.
attribute_control_button_layout: Two rotary dials and two push buttons arranged vertically on the bottom right of the front panel.
